cluster.title: Logistical Mobility & Lifecycle Logic — Kefiw
cluster.subhead: The big life-structure decisions — move, retrain, cut, reconnect — run through closed-form math.
cluster.intro: When to relocate (break-even on moving cost), when to retrain (payback including opportunity cost), when to purge subscriptions (lifetime cost compounds), and which relationships actually pay out (credit vs debit on a visual balance).
cluster.description: Geographic arbitrage, upskill ROI, subscription purge, and social utility — closed-form math for the major life-structure decisions.

faq.a: Each addresses a different axis of life logistics — geography, skill, spending, people. Together they cover the big "am I in the right configuration" decisions without overlapping scopes.

faq.q: How does this connect to the Finance suite?
faq.a: Subscription Purge pipes its monthly recovery into Runway Zero directly — cutting $50/mo extends your bankruptcy date by weeks. Upskill ROI complements Minimum Viable Rate. Geo Arbitrage pairs with Runway Zero for the "where should I live" calculation.

tools.title: Geographic Arbitrage — Migration Break-Even Calculator | Kefiw

current.description: Calculate the break-even month for a move. Monthly gain = (income − cost) at destination minus origin. Horizon visual with plane marker.
current.intro: Moving is a one-time expense that pays back in monthly surplus gains. Break-even = move cost / monthly gain. If you won't stay past break-even, the move is a bet against yourself. This tool plots the plane along a 36-month horizon.
current.outcomeLine: Break-even month for relocating · monthly surplus delta vs one-time move cost.
howTo: Enter monthly income and cost at both locations, plus one-time move cost.
Break-even = move cost / monthly gain. Negative gain = no break-even, ever.
Under 6 months = fast · 6–18 = reasonable · 18+ = only if you commit to staying.

examples.title: Equal surpluses · $6000 move
examples.body: Monthly gain $0 · No break-even · the move is pure optionality cost.

faq.a: Roll them into cost-there. Tax differential is often the biggest line item — factor in effective rate, not marginal.
Yes — keep income flat, drop cost-there to the new location, move cost to one-time relocation. The classic "remote to cheaper city" case.

tools.title: Upskill ROI — Bootcamp Payback Calculator | Kefiw

current.description: Calculate the true payback window on a course or bootcamp, including opportunity cost of study hours. Recovery progress bar with 24-month reference.
current.intro: Every "free" course costs your hourly rate × study hours. Payback = (course + opportunity cost) / monthly salary lift. Under 18 months is a solid investment; over 36 months depends on whether the skill holds value that long.
current.outcomeLine: True course ROI including study-hour opportunity cost · 1- and 3-year net.
howTo: Enter course cost, total study hours, your current hourly wage, and expected monthly salary lift.
Under 6 months = alpha bet · 6–18 = solid · 36+ = slow · no lift = negative ROI.

examples.title: $1500 course · 120h · $35/h · $400 lift
examples.body: Opportunity cost $4200 · Total $5700 · Payback 14.3 mo · Solid_Investment.

examples.title: $0 course · 200h · $50/h · $100 lift
examples.body: Opportunity cost $10000 · Payback 100 mo · Negative_ROI — find a cheaper path.

faq.q: Is study time really opportunity cost?
faq.a: If you could have billed those hours, yes. If you studied during idle time you would have wasted anyway, no. Be honest with yourself.
Skills can unlock optionality, network, and work quality — real but hard to price. This tool captures the financial floor; everything above is bonus.

tools.title: Subscription Purge — Leak Detection Calculator | Kefiw

current.description: List every recurring subscription, see the monthly bleed and the 10-year lifetime cost. Sinking-ship visual with drip animations. Pipes recovery into Runway Zero.
current.intro: Subscriptions compound silently — $15/mo looks small until you see $1800 over ten years. This tool stacks your lines, sums the monthly bleed, and projects the 5- and 10-year lifetime cost. Every dollar cut here extends the runway on the Runway Zero calculator.
current.outcomeLine: Subscription bleed · monthly, annual, 5- and 10-year lifetime cost.
howTo: Add every active subscription (streaming, cloud, apps, memberships) with monthly cost.
Monthly bleed = Σ(line). Lifetime = monthly × 12 × years.
Under $25/mo = minimal · $75–150 = serious · $300+ = taking on water.
Pipe monthly recovery into the Runway Zero calculator — every $50 cut extends the bankruptcy date by weeks.

examples.title: 15 subs @ $20/mo avg
examples.body: Monthly $300 · 10-year $36000 · Hull_Breach — kill the bottom half now.

faq.a: Divide by 12 and enter as monthly. The math is the same; the psychology is different — annual feels cheaper but compounds identically.
Without inflation adjustment it's conservative. The real 10-year cost is worse once prices creep, which most subscriptions do annually.

tools.title: Social Utility — Relationship ROI Calculator | Kefiw

current.description: Weigh support and positive energy against conflict and drain. Balance-scale visual with tilt indicator. Signals pattern, not judgement.
current.intro: Not every relationship needs to be net-positive — but the sum of your relationships must be, or you run at a deficit. This tool weighs the credit side (support + positive energy) against the debit side (conflict + drain) on a visual balance.
current.outcomeLine: Support + positive weighed against conflict + drain — utility ratio with balance tilt.
howTo: Rate four dimensions 0–10: support given, positive energy, conflict cost, energy drain.
Use it to flag patterns — then decide whether to adjust frequency, have a hard conversation, or create distance.

faq.a: It's a mirror, not a verdict. The point is to surface patterns you've been ignoring — not to grade anyone. The feeling of resisting this exercise often tells you more than the numbers.

faq.q: Does a low score mean I should end the relationship?
faq.a: No. It means something is worth addressing. Distance, boundaries, and honest conversation all work before total removal — most relationships can be repaired or rebalanced if you actually try.

enhancement_brief.goal: Lift each article from short-form summary to authority-grade long-form. Target 1,200+ words per article while keeping the structured fields crisp.
per_article_outputs: 1. Fill `enhanced` with any structured-field overrides (usually: tighter intro, expanded keyPoints, more examples, richer FAQ).
2. Fill `longform_markdown` with 800-1200 word prose body (markdown, H2 sections only — no H1).
3. Do not modify `current` — that is the baseline snapshot for diffing.

rules: Each question must read like a real search query — not marketing-speak.
First sentence of answer is a complete standalone 15-25 word definition/answer (Google quotes it directly).
Full answer 40-60 words. Tight. Front-load the answer.
Cover distinct intents: definition, comparison, edge-case, how-to, troubleshooting. Not 3 rewordings of the same angle.
Include one target keyword in at least half the questions.
Add a faq_intent tag per entry: "definition" | "comparison" | "edge-case" | "how-to" | "troubleshooting" | "cost" | "trust"
faq_rules.target_count: 5-7 entries per article, 4-6 per tool page

structure: ## H2 section 1 — deeper "what is it" / definition with context
## H2 section 2 — the mechanic / how it works (with one small example)
## H2 section 3 — when to use vs when to avoid
## H2 section 4 — worked example or walkthrough
## H2 section 6 — tool-specific tips (link out to the tool)
longform_rules.tone: Concrete, useful, first-person-plural light. No filler. No "in conclusion". Short paragraphs.
links: Every time you mention a sibling tool in this cluster, link it with the exact URL from this brief (tools[].url).
Every time you mention a sibling article, link it with the exact URL from this brief (articles[].url).
Do not invent URLs or link to external competitors.
forbidden: Medical/legal/financial advice language ("you should", "we recommend") — use neutral framing.
Generic SEO boilerplate ("in today's fast-paced world").

internal_linking.rule: Use only URLs that appear in this JSON. Prefer linking to sibling tools/articles in THIS cluster over outbound links.

quality_gates: No fabricated facts — if uncertain, phrase as "in practice" or remove.
Every H2 section earns its space — cut if it restates the intro.
Zero AI-tells: "it is important to note", "in conclusion", "let us delve", "furthermore".
